questions.explanation: DevOps focuses on collaboration, automation, and feedback loops to improve deployment frequency, reliability, and time-to-recovery.

options.A: Increase collaboration and automate delivery to ship changes faster and more reliably
options.C: Eliminate the need for testing by deploying directly to production
options.D: Move all applications to a single cloud provider

questions.question: What is the primary goal of DevOps in a software delivery organization?

questions.explanation: git checkout -b <branch> creates the branch and checks it out in one step.
questions.question: Which Git command creates a new branch named "feature/login" and switches to it?

questions.explanation: A Dockerfile is a build recipe that describes the steps and metadata needed to produce a Docker image.

options.A: Define how to build a container image (layers, commands, files, and configuration)
options.B: Store runtime logs for a container

questions.question: In Docker, what is the main purpose of a Dockerfile?

questions.explanation: top provides a real-time view of processes, CPU/memory usage, and system load.
questions.question: Which Linux command is commonly used to view running processes in real time?

questions.explanation: Automated tests plus controlled promotion (gates/approvals) reduce risk and prevent unverified changes from reaching production.

options.A: Run unit/integration tests and require approval gates before production deployment
options.B: Deploy every commit directly to production to get faster feedback
options.C: Skip tests in CI to reduce pipeline time
options.D: Only run tests on developer laptops

questions.question: In a CI/CD pipeline, which practice best ensures only validated code is deployed to production?

questions.explanation: A Service provides a stable virtual IP/DNS name and load-balances traffic to matching Pods.
questions.question: In Kubernetes, which object is typically used to expose a set of Pods internally and provide stable service discovery?

questions.explanation: Secrets should be sourced from a secrets manager or secure runtime inputs; marking variables sensitive helps reduce exposure in output, while avoiding plaintext in VCS.

options.A: Hardcode the password in a variable default value
options.B: Store the password in Terraform state only and share state publicly
options.C: Read the password from a secrets manager (e.g., Vault/SSM/Key Vault) via data sources or environment variables and mark variables as sensitive
options.D: Base64-encode the password in the .tf file

questions.question: In Terraform, what is the best way to prevent a database password from being stored in plaintext in configuration files and accidentally committed to Git?

questions.explanation: Multi-stage builds keep only runtime artifacts in the final image, and BuildKit-style secret mounts avoid persisting tokens in layers.

options.A: Use a single-stage Docker build and copy the entire source tree into the final image
options.B: Use multi-stage builds and pass secrets at build time (e.g., BuildKit secrets) without copying them into the final stage
options.C: Store secrets in the Dockerfile as ENV variables so they are available at runtime
options.D: Run the application directly on the Kubernetes node without a container

questions.question: Your CI pipeline builds a Docker image and deploys to Kubernetes. You want to minimize image size and reduce the risk of leaking build-time secrets (e.g., npm tokens). Which approach is best?

questions.explanation: Assuming roles via STS with OIDC provides short-lived, least-privilege credentials and avoids managing long-lived static keys.

options.A: Create an IAM user and store its access keys in the repository
options.B: Use AWS STS to assume an IAM role with short-lived credentials (e.g., via OIDC federation from the CI system)
options.C: Share the root account credentials with the CI system
options.D: Disable bucket policies and make the bucket public

questions.question: You need to grant a CI job permission to upload artifacts to an AWS S3 bucket without using long-lived access keys. What is the most secure and cloud-native approach?

questions.explanation: Prometheus/Grafana are well-suited for latency percentiles and alerting, while EFK/ELK centralizes logs for correlation and troubleshooting.

options.A: Prometheus for metrics/alerts + Grafana for dashboards + centralized logging with EFK/ELK for log correlation
options.B: Only EFK/ELK, because logs can replace metrics and alerting
options.C: Only Prometheus, because metrics can replace logs entirely

questions.question: A Node.js/Express API deployed on Kubernetes shows intermittent latency spikes. You want to correlate application logs with request traces and also alert on high p95 latency. Which stack best fits these needs?
